# Bird's-eye product design SSOT: the "what happened this week" digest surface

## Context
The operator's completeness audit (2026-07-04) confirmed tonight's epic-by-epic finding: Bird's-eye has SUBSTRATE ticketed (#12679's temporal pyramid + the #14433-#14435 window/velocity subs) but the PRODUCT — the operator's original "what happened this week" digest ask, the #11375 design space's consumer surface — has **no design SSOT and no render leaves**. Substrate without a drawn product is the exact gap class the sprint kills.

## The Problem
The digest is the highest-leverage CONSUMER of the temporal pyramid: an operator-legible, period-scoped narrative surface (this week: merged, graduated, shipped, stalled, direction-shift) rendered from SUMMARY_* tiers + the work graph — the thing a returning human reads in 60 seconds instead of archaeology. Undesigned, every substrate leaf builds blind.

## The Architectural Reality
Consumes: ADR-0028 window tiers (#14434 velocity fields), the #14565 direction annotations (advisory, notAuthority), #14447 stall classes, the freshness/authority ledger discipline (ADR-0032 §2.2 applies to any rendered claim). Render home candidates: the agentos module (one-app ruling) or the handoff surface first. Design bar: the cockpit-plan SSOT precedent; light+dark from birth (sibling theme ticket).

## The Fix
One design artifact (HTML mock, committed under apps/agentos/design/): the digest's IA (period selector, the five narrative blocks, drill-down affordances), rendered-claim provenance treatment (stale-as-stale visible), and the 60-second-scan bar — reviewed before any render leaf files.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Committed artifact at the house bar; light+dark comps included.
- [ ] Every rendered-claim class shows its ledger treatment (sourceAuthority/observedAt visible or summoned).
- [ ] Consumes-map table: which pyramid/direction/stall fields feed each block (substrate-anchored, no invented fields).
- [ ] Peer + operator review recorded.

## Out of Scope
Render implementation (leaves file after this passes) · substrate changes (#12679's subs proceed independently).

## Related
#12679 (substrate epic — natural parent) · #11375 (design space) · #14433-#14435 · #14565 · ADR-0028/0032. First-claim open (Ada holds #11375 authorship gravity; not assigned). Sweeps: epic list + birds-eye search at 04:02Z — no equivalent.

### @neo-opus-ada - 2026-07-12T09:03:57Z

## Lane opened — the substrate-anchored consumes-map (AC #3), design-first

Claiming this + starting where the ticket says the risk is: *"substrate without a drawn product… every substrate leaf builds blind."* The consumes-map is the contract that keeps the digest's five narrative blocks bound to **real** fields — no invented ones. V-B-A'd against live `dev`:

- `ai/services/memory-core/helpers/temporalSummaryAggregationEngine.mjs` → `deriveVelocityFields` (the six deterministic fields) + `VELOCITY_FIELD_SOURCES` (each field's named source substrate) + `WINDOW_SCOPED_VELOCITY_FIELDS` (the null-on-per-agent honesty rule).
- `ai/graph/directionVelocity.mjs` / `directionSchema.mjs` → the per-direction `{v, s, r}` breakdown (directionBreakdown / stallBreakdown per window, #14811).

### Consumes-map — narrative block → substrate field(s)

| Digest block (60-sec scan) | Substrate field(s) — verified, not invented | Source substrate | Scope |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Merged** (PRs that landed) | `mergedPrs` | GitHub PR sync — merged-PR graph nodes in window | window-scoped (null per-agent) |
| **Shipped** (code that moved dev) | `devCommits` + `mergedPrs` | git first-parent log over the window on `dev` | window-scoped |
| **Graduated** (ideas that became authority) | `sandboxesGraduated` + `adrsLanded` | Discussion graduation markers · landed ADRs (decisions dir / graph nodes) | window-scoped |
| **Stalled** (what's stuck) | direction `s` (stallBreakdown) per window (#14811) + the stall classes (#14447) | direction layer / graph | window-scoped |
| **Direction-shift** (where the swarm turned) | direction `{v, s, r}` breakdown + the #14565 direction annotations (`advisory`, `notAuthority`) | `ai/graph/directionVelocity.mjs` | window-scoped, advisory |
| **Who was active** (context strip, not a headline) | `sessionsPerAgent` + `highImpactSessions` (impact ≥ 90) | Memory Core session nodes / impact metadata | **agent-attributable** (the only two) |

### Two design invariants the map forces (both are ledger discipline, not decoration)

1. **The honest-null rule is visible in the render.** `WINDOW_SCOPED_VELOCITY_FIELDS` are `null` — not `0`, not the repeated window count — on per-agent partitions. So the digest MUST NOT show a per-agent "merged: 0"; whole-repo blocks (Merged/Shipped/Graduated/Stalled/Direction-shift) render on the unified track, and only the "who was active" strip is per-agent. This kills the silent-double-count class at the design bar, before a render leaf can seed it.
2. **Rendered-claim provenance (ADR-0032 §2.2).** Every block shows `sourceAuthority` / `observedAt` (visible or summoned), and direction-shift renders as **advisory** (`notAuthority`) — never as a certainty verdict. Stale-as-stale is visible, per AC #2.

### Next slices (this leaf stays design-only, per the ticket's Out-of-Scope)

1. Confirm the exact stall-class field name (#14447) + the `directionVelocity` public shape at mock time (flagged here rather than invented).
2. The committed HTML artifact under `apps/agentos/design/`: period selector, the five blocks + the activity strip, drill-down affordances, light+dark from birth (house bar / cockpit-plan SSOT precedent), provenance treatment on every rendered claim.
3. Peer + operator review (AC #4) before any render leaf files.
